channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: 120kN Engine Production Could Seed India’s Next-Generation Fighter Engine Breakthrough
item.description: India’s long pursuit of aero-engine self-reliance may be approaching an inflection point, with local production of advanced Western fighter engines poised to do more than just power aircraft. According to sources familiar with ongoing programmes, domestic manufacturing of engines in the M88 and F414 class is expected to create the industrial and technological ecosystem needed for India to take a decisive step toward developing a next-generation adaptive or variable cycle engine — the kind of propulsion architecture required for future sixth-generation combat aircraft.
item.dc:creator: RAUNAK KUNDE

item.title: Indian Army Pushes Indigenous 155mm Terminally Guided Munition for “One-Shot, One-Kill” Anti-Tank Precision
item.description: The Indian Army has initiated a decisive push toward next-generation precision artillery with a high-priority program to develop an indigenous 155mm Terminally Guided Munition (TGM), aimed at delivering a true “one-shot, one-kill” capability against moving armored targets. This effort marks a doctrinal shift in artillery employment, transforming conventional tube artillery into a precision strike asset capable of neutralizing high-value targets with minimal rounds and reduced collateral exposure.
item.dc:creator: RAUNAK KUNDE

item.title: IAF to Replace Legacy SARBE Radios with Indigenous SDR-HH for Fighter Pilots
item.description: The Indian Air Force is transitioning from legacy survival radio systems to an advanced, fully indigenous Software Defined Radio–Handheld (SDR-HH), marking a critical upgrade in combat search-and-rescue (CSAR) capability and pilot survivability. Developed jointly by Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) and DRDO, the new system represents a shift from basic distress beacons to network-centric, encrypted communication nodes embedded within India’s broader air defence architecture.
item.dc:creator: RAUNAK KUNDE

item.title: AMCA’s Private Turn: Why HAL’s Exclusion Signals a Structural Reset in India’s Fighter Aircraft Manufacturing Model
item.description: The decision to exclude Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) from the AMCA prototype phase in early 2026 marks a structural inflection point rather than a routine programmatic shift. For the first time since independence, India is consciously decoupling fighter aircraft design from its traditional state-run manufacturing pipeline and entrusting prototype development to a private-sector-led Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV). This is not a verdict on HAL’s legacy—it remains the backbone of India’s combat aviation ecosystem—but a recognition that fifth-generation aircraft demand a fundamentally different industrial architecture, one that prioritizes agility, concurrency in design and manufacturing, and risk-sharing.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Uniting India’s Skies: Why the IAF and Navy Must Merge AMCA and TEDBF Programs to Secure Indigenous Airpower Leadership
item.description: In the high-stakes theater of modern aerial warfare, India’s defense planners face a pivotal crossroads: consolidate the Indian Air Force’s (IAF) Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) and the Indian Navy’s Twin Engine Deck Based Fighter (TEDBF) into a single, versatile family of platforms, or risk a repeat of history where foreign imports sideline homegrown innovation. As the AMCA prototype nears rollout and the Navy eyes nuclear-powered carriers equipped with Electromagnetic Aircraft Launch Systems (EMALS), experts warn that without urgent integration—encompassing unmanned wingmen and carrier ecosystems—procurements like the looming 114 Rafale jets could crowd out the AMCA, much as they once overshadowed the Tejas program.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Sudarshan Chakra: India’s Multi-Layered Air Defense Shield to Counter Evolving Threats
item.description: In his Independence Day speech on August 15, 2025, Prime Minister Narendra Modi introduced the nation to Mission Sudarshan Chakra, a transformative air defense architecture designed to safeguard India’s skies from a spectrum of aerial threats. Described by the Hindustan Times as a complex integration of long-range radars, surveillance inputs from satellites, aircraft, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and long-range interceptor missiles, Sudarshan Chakra represents India’s most ambitious effort to create a multi-layered air defense network.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Aditya Precitech Develops Indigenous Autonomous ISR Rover as India Expands UGV Capabilities
item.description: Hyderabad-based Aditya Precitech Pvt. Ltd. is making a strategic transition from a precision manufacturing firm to a defence systems innovator with the development of an Autonomous ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance) Rover, as India accelerates its push toward indigenous unmanned ground vehicle (UGV) capabilities.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: DRDO & Indian Navy successfully conduct maiden salvo launch of Naval Anti-ship Missile-Short Range
item.description: All test objectives were fully met as per the data captured using various range tracking instruments like radar, electro-optical system and telemetry deployed by the Integrated Test Range (ITR), Chandipur. Along with proving the salvo launch capability, the missiles demonstrated the waterline hit capability. The test launches were witnessed by senior scientists from DRDO, Users’ representatives from the Indian Navy and the Indian Air Force and Development-cum-Production Partners (DcPP).
item.dc:creator: PIB

item.title: Russia’s RusBeam 2800 3D printer delivered to ISRO that can Print titanium alloy for India’s aerospace dreams
item.description: It prints at a blistering 50 millimetres per second — producing a 50-kilogram component in just five hours. It can create towering structures up to 2.8 metres high and weighing as much as four tonnes, all within a carefully controlled vacuum, guided by custom-designed software built specifically for its Indian partners.
item.dc:creator: USI

item.title: NFC-Kota has received operational clearance from the Atomic Energy Regulatory Board
item.description: Delivering fuel and core components for PHWRs since inception, NFC continues to power India’s growth. NFC-Kota reflects the spirit of Nuclear Energy Mission, advancing clean, reliable, and self-reliant energy for a ViksitBharat.
item.dc:creator: USI

item.title: Defence collaboration, industrial partnership in focus during Crosetto’s Delhi visit
item.description: “Defence Minister Guido Crosetto has traveled to India, where tomorrow he will meet his counterpart Shri Rajnath Singh. The Ministers will discuss a series of topics related to cooperation in the defence sector between our countries. An exchange of views is also planned on the main regional and global issues, in light of the evolving security landscape,” read a statement issued by Italy’s Ministry of Defence.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Russian FM Lavrov to visit India next week for BRICS meet, hold talks
item.description: “On May 14-15, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov will attend a full-format meeting of BRICS foreign ministers in New Delhi,” Zakharova said.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Punjab Police foil terror plots, seize RPG and arms cache
item.description: The recovered military hardware includes one rocket-propelled grenade, one metallic improvised explosive device (sticky bomb) weighing 2.296 kg, along with three detonators with electric wire and one battery, two packs of RDX weighing 1.456 kg, one P-86 hand grenade, three pistols, 9 mm Glock-18 CX ‘Austria’, .30 bore PX5 Storm and .30 bore Star Mark, along with five magazines and 84 cartridges, two wireless sets Baofeng, one headphone, and two timer switches.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: India Expands Doppler Weather Radar Network By Over 250 Pc Since 2014: Dr Jitendra Singh
item.description: Speaking to the media after inaugurating a Selfie Point depicting a weather radar at the India Meteorological Department (IMD) headquarters in the national capital, Dr Jitendra Singh said the expansion now covers more than 87 per cent of the country and has substantially improved forecasting of cyclones, heavy rainfall and thunderstorms.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Will encourage King Charles to return Kohinoor diamond to India: Mamdani
item.description: King Charles addressed a joint meeting of the US Congress Tuesday, as he and Queen Camilla were honoured at a state dinner hosted by President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump in the White House.
item.dc:creator: PTI
